Job.JobDescription: Albuquerque Public Schools is looking for a Volunteer Assistant Coach to join our program. Apply now.

Farmington Municipal Schools is looking for a Volleyball Strength and Conditioning to join our program. Apply now.
Position: Head Varsity Coach Supervisor: Principal – Athletic Director General Job Description: The Head Varsity Coach will supervise practice, contests, travel, equipment and other requirements related to the sport they are coaching. They are to ensure student safety and eligibility for participating in the sport. Essential Duties and Responsibilities: Maintain an accurate and updated list of supplies and equipment. Submit a copy of the season wrap-up form to the Athletic Director within two (2) weeks of the last contest; include major equipment/uniform needs Check in equipment at the end of the season. Charge students for lost equipment. Store and secure uniforms, equipment, and supplies within two (2) weeks of the last contest. Ensure that each participant has a physical examination form turned in to the office prior to participating in practice. Collect the insurance forms and training rules before the first practice. Submit copy of policies to the Athletic Director Organize, conduct, and supervise practices throughout the season. Notify Athletic Office of practice schedules and changes in practice schedules. Clear athletes from the gymnasium, locker room, and weight room before leaving the building. See that participants do not loiter around the building or in the halls immediately after practice Supervise students during practices and games inclusive of locker room, gymnasium and gym lobby Ride the bus to and from all contests Require that participants maintain acceptable standards of behavior and decorum Provide team and player publicity to local media Attend end-of-season awards banquet and present season awards Establish written expectations for assistants. Provide a copy of these expectations to the coaching staff and administrators Develop comprehensive programs for the off season Check eligibility each grading period. Discrepancies are resolved through the Athletic Director Provide all necessary information on time to appropriate offices (i.e., excused absence list prior to departure) Accept other responsibilities and duties as deemed necessary by supervisor Qualifications: Appropriate National and/or State Coaching Certifications. Physical Requirements: Sitting, standing, lifting and carrying (up to 50 pounds), reaching, squatting, kneeling, prolonged typing, physical ability to type on a keyboard terminal, and moving equipment may be required. Equipment/Technology Handled: Must know how to properly operate or be willing to learn to operate equipment related to the sport, computer and other equipment as needed. Work Environment: Must be able to work within various degrees of noise, temperature, and air quality. Interruptions of work are routine. Flexibility and patience are required. Must be self-motivated and able to complete job assignment without direct supervision. After hour work may be required. Terms of Employment: Salary and work calendar established yearly by the district.

Must be self-motivated and able to complete job assignment without direct supervision. After hour work may be required. Terms of Employment: Salary and work calendar established yearly by the district.

Roswell Independent School District Job Description Job Title: HEAD COACH Reports To: ATHLETIC COORDINATOR – HIGH SCHOOL/DIRECTOR OF ATHLETICS/ACTIVITIES General Job Description: Responsible for all aspects of the Athletic program for their respective sport. Essential Duties and Responsibilities: Has knowledge of existing system and State and NMAA regulations. Implements it consistently and interprets them for the staff. Has a thorough knowledge of the entire athletic policy handbook approved by the Roswell Independent School District Board of Education and is responsible for its implementation by the entire staff of the sports program. Provides strong leadership for assistants and athletes in the program. Advises the activities director and recommends policy, method or procedural changes. Provides proper safeguards for maintenance and protection of assigned equipment sites. Assists the athletic director in scheduling, providing transportation, and requirements for tournaments and special sport events. Provides documentation to fulfill state and system requirements concerning physical examinations, parental consent and eligibility. Maintain discipline, adjusts grievances, and works to increase morale and cooperation. Delegates specific duties, supervises implementation, and at season’s end, analyze staff effectiveness and evaluates all assistants. Established the fundamental philosophy, skills and techniques to be taught by staff. Designs conferences, clinics and staff meetings to insure staff awareness of over-all program. Trains and informs staff and encourages professional growth by encouraging clinic attendance according to local clinic policy. Presents information to news media concerning schedules, tournaments and results. Responsible for the quality, effectiveness and validity of any oral or written release to local media. Responsible for maintaining good public relations with news media, booster clubs, parents, officials, volunteers and fans. Promotes the sport within the school through recruiting athletes that are not in another sports program and promotes the sport outside the school through news media, Little League programs or any other feasible manner. Determine student discipline, delineates procedures concerning due process when the enforcement of discipline is necessary. Gives constant attention to a student athlete’s grades and conduct. Assists athletes in their college or advanced educational selection. Directs student managers, assistants and statisticians. Completes paperwork on all disabling athletic injuries on proper forms and submits to the athletic office within 24 hours. Provides training rules and any other unique regulations of the sport to each athletic who is considered a participant. By his/her presence at all practices, games and while traveling provides assistance, guidance and safeguards for each participant. Initiates programs and policies concerning injuries, medical attention and emergencies. Is accountable for all equipment and collects the cost of any equipment lost or not returned. Arranges for issuing, storing, and reconditioning of equipment and submits annual inventory and current records concerning this equipment. Monitors equipment rooms and coaches offices, and authorizes who may enter issue or requisition equipment. Permits the athletes to only be in authorized areas of the building at the appropriate times. Instills in each player a respect for equipment and school property, its care and its proper usage. Maintain confidentiality with sensitive matters. Be flexible and able to prioritize tasks and maintain accurate and detailed records. Page 1 of 2 Roswell Independent School District HEAD COACH (CONT’D) Report to work on time and evening/weekend hours are applicable. Attempt to deescalate students/parents/staff; listening/visiting with them about their concerns prior to them meeting with administrators. May be required to perform other duties/functions as directed by supervisor. Supervisory Responsibilities: Assistant Coaches Qualifications: High School diploma or GED. Current NM Coaching license including CPR/AED Certification. Valid Driver’s license and Car Insurance. Ability to drive from site to site as needed.

Roswell Independent School District Job Description Job Title: ASSISTANT COACH Reports To: HEAD COACH/PRINCIPAL/DIRECTOR OF ATHLETICS/ACTIVITIES Essential Duties and Responsibilities: Has knowledge of existing system and State and NMAA regulations. Has a thorough knowledge of all the athletic policies approved by the Roswell Independent School District Board of Education and is responsible for their implementation. Has knowledge of the existing system, state and league regulations and implements them consistently. Maintains discipline and works to increase morale and cooperate within the school sports program. Recommends to the head coach budgetary items for next year in his area of the program. Monitors equipment rooms and coaches offices and authorizes who may enter. Is accountable to the head coach for all equipment. Collects the cost of any equipment lost or not returned and arranges for issuing and storing of equipment and submits to the head coach an annual inventory and current records concerning this equipment. Examines locker rooms before and after practices and games, checking on general cleanliness of the facility. Responsible for cleanliness and maintenance of the specific equipment. Instills in each player a respect for equipment and school property, its care and proper usage. Assist in the necessary preparation to hold scheduled sports events or practices and adheres to scheduled facility times. Coordinates program with maintenance and school employees. Provides proper safeguards for maintenance and protection of assigned equipment sites. Assists the head coach in scheduling, providing transportation to tournaments and special sporting events. Directs student managers and statisticians on respective games. Provides training rules and any other unique regulation of the sport to each athlete who is considered a participant. Determines student discipline, delineates procedures concerning due process when the enforcement of discipline is necessary. By his presence at all practices, games and while traveling, provides assistance and guidance to and safeguards for each participant. Instructs team members as to changes in the rules and teaches fundamentals of the sport as outlined by the head coach. Assists the head coach in carrying out his responsibility. Attends all staff meetings and carries out scouting assignments as outlined by the head coach. Works within the basic framework and philosophy of the head coach of that sport. Arrives early enough before practice, contests and meetings to adequately prepare and remains long enough afterwards to help players with problems or to become involved in staff discussions. Never criticizes, admonishes or argues with head coach or any staff member within ears or eyes of players or parents. Helps in the planning and implementation of both in-season and out-or-season conditioning and weight programs as is possible. Attends most of the contests, when possible, or other teams in the program. Strives to improve skills by attending clinics and using resources made available by the head coach. Performs such other duties that are consistent with the nature of the position and that may be requested by the head coach. Maintain confidentiality with sensitive matters. Be flexible and able to prioritize tasks and maintain accurate and detailed records. Report to work on time and evening /weekend hours are applicable. May be required to perform other duties/functions as directed by supervisor. Job.JobTitle: Coach Assistant Boys Basketball High School Posting 550
Job.JobDescription:
Los Alamos Public Schools is looking for a Basketball Assistant Coach to join our program. Apply now.
Visa Sponsorship is not available Successful experience as either a head or assistant coach, or successful experience as a player or coach at the college level. To direct the planning, preparations and coaching activities of the boys basketball program from the mid-school level to the high school. To plan, schedule and conduct a regular program of practice and competition in accordance with rules of the school, the NMAA, and District 2-4A. To assist the Athletic Director and Athletic Trainer in the distribution and collection of appropriate information i.e. Physical forms, and all other forms that are required by the NMAA, District 2-4A and the high school. To attend the appropriate meetings pertaining to basketball as scheduled by the NMAA or District 2-4A. To recommend purchase of equipment, supplies and uniforms as needed. To assume responsibility for maintaining an accurate inventory of equipment pertaining to the sport. To oversee the safety and conditions of the facility or area assigned to the boys basketball programs. To assume responsibility for supervision, safety and welfare of students participating in the basketball program before, during and after scheduled practices and competitive contests. To establish and maintain good rapport with athletes, staff, parents, community groups and news media. To assume responsibility for submitting request for transportation forms to the Athletic Director. To assist the Athletic Director in planning and organization of promotion and participant recruitment and recognition programs pertaining to the sport. To perform assigned tasks in a timely and efficient manner. To perform assigned tasks with a high standard of quality. To perform other duties as assigned by the Principal or designee.
Job.CompanyDescription: Los Alamos Public Schools coaching jobs

Job.JobTitle: Coach Track and Field High School Posting 549
Job.JobDescription:
Los Alamos Public Schools is looking for a Track & Field coach to join our program. Apply now.
Visa Sponsorship is not available To provide leadership, supervision and set examples for the High School T&F program. Stay knowledgeable of NMAA, District 2-4A, and Los Alamos, rules and regulations. Attend clinics whenever possible to learn new ideas and to stay informed. Inventory and maintain equipment. Explain what is expected fortrack athletes in and out of competition. Work in conjunction with other Coaches. Make sure every athlete has a physical on file. Turn in all paperwork in a timely manner. Apply discipline in a firm, fair and positive manner. Make sure athletes conduct themselves in a proper manner on and off the field. Make sure team treats opponents and officials with respect. Keep the Athletic Director informed at all times about the program. Be able to work in cooperation with all your coaching staff and parents. Must possess or be willing to acquire a NM PED Coaching License. Other courses also required. Must be able to demonstrate the sport or activity, including lifting all equipment as required by the activity. This is to include games and practices. The employee must have the physical stamina to be able to handle the hours and physical stress of the sport during contests and practices. The employee should be able to use the machines, tools, equipment, and work aids which may be representative of those commonly associated with this type of work.

Wappingers Central S.D. is looking for a coach to join our program. Apply now.
DISTINGUISHING FEATURES OF THE CLASS: Provides for the care, prevention and treatment of athletic injuries and/or improves physical fitness of athletes for participation in various sports programs. Under the general supervision of a licensed physician and the District Athletic Director, incumbents of this position are responsible for the care and prevention and treatment of athletic injuries involving student athletes. In accordance with Section 8532 of New York State Education Law the practice of the profession of athletic training is defined as the application of principles and methods of conditioning athletes, preventing and treating athletic injuries through the use of appropriate preventative and supportive devices, under the direction of a physician, recognizing and evaluating athletic injuries, administering first aid and emergency care, developing and implementing treatment and rehabilitation programs for athletic injuries under the direction of a physician and the planning and implementation of programs and services to help prevent athletic injuries. Incumbents are responsible for the record keeping of athletic injuries and treatments. Does related work as required.

Jenks Public Schools is looking for a Lacrosse Assistant Coach to join our program. Apply now.
Jenks Public Schools Job Description Position: Assistant Coach - High School Boys Lacrosse Reports to: Director of Community Education and Assistant Director – Athletics, Community Education Supervises: None Qualifications: High School diploma or GED required. Has previous success in assigned coaching position. Extensive knowledge, skills and ability in the strategy and technology of the sport. Experience interacting with student athletes, parents, and assistant coaches through deliberate positive interactions. Ability to serve as an effective role model for student athletes and represent Jenks Public Schools and Jenks Community Education with integrity and respect. Satisfy all district requirements with regard to physical health and citizenship. Such alternatives to the above qualifications as the Board may find appropriate and acceptable. Position Goal: Assist with all aspects of student-athlete development and performance of the lacrosse program. Essential Functions and Responsibilities: Assist in the planning, development, and the execution of the high school boys lacrosse program by focusing on building a positive culture and developing quality relationships with student athletes and parents. Assist in the implementation of sound practices and skill development that maximizes student athlete performance in lacrosse in collaboration with the head coach. Communicate effectively with all program stakeholders to ensure a positive, quality experience is provided for our student athletes. Collaborate with the coordinator, assistant coordinator, and the Booster Club. Assist in providing direction and evaluation of youth coaches and teams to develop vertical alignment throughout the program. Employment Terms: Community Education Classified - Temporary Contract FLSA Qualification: Non-Exempt Evaluation: None

Job.JobTitle: 1.0 FTE Head Men's Volleyball Coach – Bend Senior High School – Regular
Job.JobDescription:
Bend Senior High School is looking for a Volleyball Head Coach to join our program. Apply now.
Description Bend-La Pine Schools is committed to the principle of equity. Equity supersedes the notion of equality, where all are treated the same. Pursuing equity requires the removal of barriers and the promotion of inclusive practices so that all students fully benefit. The principle of equity will inform all BLS policies, regulations, programs, operations, practices, and resource allocations. Studies have shown that some individuals (women and people of color, for example) are less likely to apply for jobs unless they believe they meet every single qualification in a job description. Our goal is to find the best candidate for the position, and we acknowledge that that candidate may be an individual from a less traditional background. We encourage you to apply, even if you don't believe you meet every one of our qualifications described. If you are unsure whether you meet the qualifications of a position, or how this would be determined, please feel free to contact Human Resources to discuss your application. Classification Title: Boys' Volleyball Coach Reports To: Athletic Director Pay: Extra Duty Salary Schedule, Range Classification Definition: This classification encompasses assignments that support co-curricular and extracurricular programming across athletics, student activities, and enrichment opportunities. These roles provide students with structured opportunities that extend beyond the core curriculum and may occur outside the traditional classroom setting, and promote skill-building, personal development, and increased belonging. Assignments under this classification are made on a year-to-year basis and may be seasonal or year long, depending on program needs.

Job.JobTitle: 1.0 FTE Boy's Tennis Head Coach - Caldera High School - Regular
Job.JobDescription:
Caldera High School is looking for a Tennis Head Coach to join our program. Apply now.
Our goal is to find the best candidate for the position, and we acknowledge that that candidate may be an individual from a less traditional background. We encourage you to apply, even if you don't believe you meet every one of our qualifications described. If you are unsure whether you meet the qualifications of a position, or how this would be determined, please feel free to contact Human Resources to discuss your application. As you prepare to submit your application for employment, please note that all consideration of experience for the position and initial salary placements will be based upon the information you submit with your original application. Resumes must be detailed and verifiable for successful experience credit, any information provided to Bend-La Pine Schools at a later date will not be the basis for adjustments in salary placement. Bend La Pine Schools Head Coaching Details Applicant must submit: 1. Letter of application detailing experience. 2. Current Resume – listing experience related to this position and working with students. 3. A minimum of three letters of recommendation. 4. A minimum of three references with contact information. General Job Qualifications and Key Responsibilities of the Head Coach: Hold high ethical and professional standards. Be an appropriate role model for high school student-athletes. Hold high expectations and accountability academically and behaviorallyfor all student-athletes and coaching staff. Ability to develop, articulate, and implement a vision for program. Ability to work in a cooperative and harmonious manner in an educationally based, school-wide athletic program. Experience leading a comprehensive high school athletic program preferred. Be relationship/process oriented. Understand and provide information regarding eligibility, tryout process, cut process, and all other team-related information to include lettering, team rules, etc. Be creative and involved in all fundraising for the program. Communicate effectively with ALL stakeholders. Be an advocate for the multi-sport athlete.
Job.Location: Caldera High School, 60925 SE 15th Street, Bend, OR, 97702, United States

Job.JobTitle: 1.0 FTE Head Men's Volleyball Coach - Summit High School - Temporary
Job.JobDescription:
Summit High School is looking for a Volleyball Head Coach to join our program. Apply now.
The Boys' Volleyball Coach is responsible for developing, organizing, and leading a comprehensive volleyball program that fosters athletic skill development, teamwork, sportsmanship, and personal growth among student-athletes. The coach plans and conducts practices, teaches fundamental and advanced techniques, and ensures a safe, positive, and inclusive environment that supports both competitive excellence and educational values. The coach works in coordination with school, district, and statewide athletic policies to manage all aspects of the program, including scheduling, communication with families, and supervision of assistant coaches and players. The Boys' Volleyball Coach serves as a mentor and role model for student-athletes, promoting academic achievement, character development, and respect within the team, school, and community.

Reading School District is looking for a Track & Field Assistant Coach to join our program. Apply now.
Position Title: High School Assistant Coach Reports To: Director of Athletics & High School Head Coach Salary: Stipend POSITION GOAL: The successful candidate will be responsible for creating a safe and positive atmosphere that welcomes and respects all individuals while promoting and maintaining safe conditions in accordance with PIAA, league, sports medicine and RSD policies/procedures. Furthermore the candidate will be responsible for assisting student-athletes to establish a life after high school while instilling Red Knight pride, connecting with youth programs, teaching education through athletics, and fundraising. QUALIFICATIONS: Must be a minimum age of 18 years old. High School Diploma or equivalent required. Preferable experience working in a supervision role in athletics, recreation, education, or related fields. Exceptional interpersonal skills including the ability to effectively communicate with adults and students in a diverse work environment. ESSENTIAL FUNCTIONS: Supervise student athletes Stay current with current rules, coaching techniques, schemes, and best practices Monitor student-athletes to prevent accidents and injuries. Place student-athletes in positions to succeed not only in their sport, but also in life Demonstrate strong interpersonal skills including communication skills with sports medicine staff, game workers, officials, media, students, parents, spectators, and facility staff. Ensures that all possible safety measures are observed, including taking proper care of equipment and proper surveillance of program participants. Attend all training sessions and any additional staff meetings. Work flexible hours including off season training, early mornings, afternoon/evenings, weekends, and holidays. Verify student-athletes are eligible by PIAA and Reading School District Athletic policies. Secure equipment and facilities after each usage Communicate with RSD admin, safe schools, custodial services and/or RPD as needed. Communicate with youth groups and foster participation growth Be a good role model to student-athletes Support all RSD athletics PHYSICAL REQUIREMENTS: Physically perform all skills required of a coach. Hear noises and distress signals in a crowded athletic facility. Able to withstand extreme weather conditions Able to lift at least 20 pounds.

Reading School District is looking for a Track & Field Assistant Coach to join our program. Apply now.
Position Title: Middle School/Freshmen Assistant Coach Reports To: Director of Athletics & High School Head Coach Salary: Stipend (set by coaches pay scale) POSITION GOAL: The successful candidate will be responsible for creating a safe and positive atmosphere that welcomes and respects all individuals while promoting and maintaining safe conditions in accordance with PIAA, league, sports medicine and RSD policies/procedures. Furthermore the candidate will be responsible for assisting student-athletes to establish a life after high school while instilling Red Knight pride, connecting with youth programs, teaching education through athletics, and fundraising. QUALIFICATIONS: Must be a minimum age of 18 years old. High School Diploma or equivalent required. Preferable experience working in a supervision role in athletics, recreation, education, or related fields. Exceptional interpersonal skills including the ability to effectively communicate with adults and students in a diverse work environment. ESSENTIAL FUNCTIONS: Supervise student athletes Stay current with current rules, coaching techniques, schemes, and best practices Monitor student-athletes to prevent accidents and injuries. Place student-athletes in positions to succeed not only in their sport, but also in life Demonstrate strong interpersonal skills including communication skills with sports medicine staff, game workers, officials, media, students, parents, spectators, and facility staff. Ensures that all possible safety measures are observed, including taking proper care of equipment and proper surveillance of program participants. Attend all training sessions and any additional staff meetings. Work flexible hours including off season training, early mornings, afternoon/evenings, weekends, and holidays. Verify student-athletes are eligible by PIAA and Reading School District Athletic policies. Secure equipment and facilities after each usage Communicate with RSD admin, safe schools, custodial services and/or RPD as needed. Communicate with youth groups and foster participation growth Be a good role model to student-athletes Support all RSD athletics PHYSICAL REQUIREMENTS: Physically perform all skills required of a coach. Hear noises and distress signals in a crowded athletic facility. Able to withstand extreme weather conditions Able to lift at least 20 pounds.

Job.JobDescription:
Providence Public Sch. is looking for a Track & Field Head Coach to join our program. Apply now.
Under direction of the school principal at Classical High School and the Athletic Director/or appropriate supervisor/ Administrator, Individuals will be available three hours each day during the entire season. This position is contingent upon the following: Preference will be given to internal/school based staff and co-op school staff. Successful recruitment of team members and ability to field a team for practice and game conditions. Proper physical conditioning related to the sport during practice sessions. Successful completion of the interscholastic season You must complete the new hire compliance training prior to your first day of employment Bilingual Preferred Selected candidate will be required to pass a pre-employment criminal history background check. Selected candidates cannot begin work until passing their background check. Salary Rate Range: 10% of Teacher Salary Scale EDUCATION REQUIREMENTS: RIDE Requirements: Must be certified in accordance with the new RIIL Coaching Permit Requirements Qualifications: BA in education or related field preferred but not required. Have current CPR/First Aid Certification in possession (required) Principles of Coaching. Psychology of Coaching. Athletic Training (Prevention and care of athletic injuries). Bio-mechanics (Analysis and understanding of human movement). Previous playing and coaching experience preferred. Dedication to the service of youth and ability to gain and hold the respect of team members. DESCRIPTION OF DUTIES & RESPONSIBILITIES: Teach fundamentals and skills. Organize practice sessions. Keep records and report results to local media. Provide Principal and Athletic Director an outline of a general practice session. Conduct coaches meetings and assign coaches responsibilities. Provide for care and prevention of injuries. Conform to all rules established by the Principal's Committee on Athletics and perform duties in accordance with the Providence School Department Athletic Handbook. Teach rules and regulations of the sport. Display good sportsmanship at all times. Responsible for the issuing, maintaining, and collecting of equipment. Prepare team budget. Recruitment of players and advancement of the sport. EEO Statement Providence Public Schools is an equal opportunity/affirmative action employer that is actively seeking to increase minority recruitment among its administrators and teaching staff. PSD does not discriminate on the basis of race, age, sex, religion, sexual orientation, gender identity or expression, national origin, color, disability or veteran status. All programs are open to all individuals, regardless of their race, age, sex, religion, sexual orientation, gender identity or expression, national origin, color, disability or veteran status. Notice of E-Verify Participation and Right to Work Providence Public School District participates in E-Verify . Federal law requires all participating employers to verify the identity and employment eligibility of all persons hired to work in the United States. Please review the following posters for more information: Notice of E-Verify Participation (English/Spanish) IER Right to Work Poster (English/Spanish)

Richland County D1 is looking for a Strength and Conditioning to join our program. Apply now.
Richland County School District One, South Carolina Classification Description Classification Title: Supplemental Coach Job Summary: We are seeking a dynamic, passionate, and skilled Supplemental Athletic Coach to assist in the development and coaching of student-athletes in various sports programs. This position will focus on enhancing individual and team performance, supporting the head coach, and promoting a positive and inclusive athletic environment. The Supplemental Athletic Coach will work closely with athletes, coaches, and staff to help guide students toward their full potential, both in sport and personal development. Key Responsibilities: · Assist the head coach with planning and implementing daily practice sessions, drills, and team strategies. · Provide individualized coaching and support to athletes to help improve their technical skills, athletic performance, and teamwork. · Foster a positive and motivating atmosphere that encourages the growth and development of all student-athletes. · Evaluate athlete performance and provide constructive feedback to improve skills, strength, and conditioning. · Support the organization and management of game-day operations, including warm-ups, player preparation, and team logistics. · Collaborate with coaching staff to set team goals and help track progress toward those goals. · Assist in the monitoring of athlete eligibility, attendance, and participation requirements. · Ensure that all practices and events comply with relevant regulations, rules, and school policies. · Help maintain and organize athletic equipment and ensure the safety of all participants during training and events. · Serve as a mentor and role model, promoting sportsmanship, leadership, and respect for others. · Communicate regularly with parents and guardians regarding athlete progress and team matters. · Contribute to the recruitment of new athletes and support the retention of current team members. Qualifications: · Previous coaching experience in the relevant sport (1-3 years preferred). · Knowledge of sport-specific techniques, rules, and strategies. · Ability to communicate effectively with athletes, parents, staff, and administration. · Strong organizational and time-management skills. · A commitment to promoting a positive and inclusive team environment. · CPR and First Aid certification (or willingness to obtain). · High school diploma or equivalent (Bachelor's degree in Physical Education, Sports Management, or a related field is a plus). · Ability to work flexible hours, including evenings and weekends, as needed. Desired Skills & Abilities: · Strong interpersonal and motivational skills to connect with athletes. · Ability to adapt coaching techniques to the needs and skill levels of individual athletes. · Experience with strength and conditioning, as well as injury prevention and rehabilitation. · Enthusiasm for teaching and mentoring young athletes. · Knowledge of current trends in sports coaching and athlete development. Physical Requirements: · Ability to participate in physical activities such as drills, conditioning, and demonstrations. · Ability to lift and carry athletic equipment and materials. · Ability to stand, walk, and move around frequently during practices and games. Working Conditions: · The position may require working outside in various weather conditions. · Some travel may be required for away games and tournaments.

Key Responsibilities: · Assist the head coach with planning and implementing daily practice sessions, drills, and team strategies. · Provide individualized coaching and support to athletes to help improve their technical skills, athletic performance, and teamwork. · Foster a positive and motivating atmosphere that encourages the growth and development of all student-athletes. · Evaluate athlete performance and provide constructive feedback to improve skills, strength, and conditioning. · Support the organization and management of game-day operations, including warm-ups, player preparation, and team logistics. · Collaborate with coaching staff to set team goals and help track progress toward those goals. · Assist in the monitoring of athlete eligibility, attendance, and participation requirements. · Ensure that all practices and events comply with relevant regulations, rules, and school policies. · Help maintain and organize athletic equipment and ensure the safety of all participants during training and events. · Serve as a mentor and role model, promoting sportsmanship, leadership, and respect for others. · Communicate regularly with parents and guardians regarding athlete progress and team matters. · Contribute to the recruitment of new athletes and support the retention of current team members. Qualifications: · Previous coaching experience in the relevant sport (1-3 years preferred). · Knowledge of sport-specific techniques, rules, and strategies. · Ability to communicate effectively with athletes, parents, staff, and administration. · Strong organizational and time-management skills. · A commitment to promoting a positive and inclusive team environment. · CPR and First Aid certification (or willingness to obtain). · High school diploma or equivalent (Bachelor's degree in Physical Education, Sports Management, or a related field is a plus). · Ability to work flexible hours, including evenings and weekends, as needed. Desired Skills & Abilities: · Strong interpersonal and motivational skills to connect with athletes. · Ability to adapt coaching techniques to the needs and skill levels of individual athletes. · Experience with strength and conditioning, as well as injury prevention and rehabilitation. · Enthusiasm for teaching and mentoring young athletes. · Knowledge of current trends in sports coaching and athlete development. Physical Requirements: · Ability to participate in physical activities such as drills, conditioning, and demonstrations. · Ability to lift and carry athletic equipment and materials. · Ability to stand, walk, and move around frequently during practices and games. Working Conditions: · The position may require working outside in various weather conditions. · Some travel may be required for away games and tournaments.

Job.JobTitle: 2026-2027 Athletic Director at Hilton Head Island High School
Job.JobDescription:
Beaufort County is looking for a coach to join our program. Apply now.
POSITION PURPOSE: To provide each enrolled student an opportunity to participate in an extracurricular athletic activity that will foster physical skills, a sense of worth and competence, a knowledge and understanding of the pleasure of sport, the principles of play, and satisfaction of being part of a team. ESSENTIAL FUNCTIONS: Organizes and administers the overall program of extracurricular athletics, both intramural and interscholastic for the assigned school. Provides leadership in the selection, assignment, and evaluation of athletic coaches and staff members. Fosters good school-community relations by keeping the community aware of and responsive to the athletic program. Promotes student athletes with post-secondary educational institutions. Assists student athletes with obtaining appropriate post-secondary educational opportunities. Assumes responsibility for the organization and scheduling of all interscholastic athletic events. Hires officials, team physicians, and security, as required, and assumes general responsibility for the proper supervision of home games. Arranges transportation for athletic contest participants. Develops and places into operation appropriate rules and regulations governing the conduct of athletic activities. Verifies each athlete’s eligibility according to established physical and academic requirements of eligibility for participation in each sport. Prepares and administers the athletic program budget. Supervises all ticket sales and fund-raising events of the athletic program and assumes responsibility for proper handling of funds. Meets and/or assists with the needs of visiting teams, such as lodging, meals, towels, gymnasium services, and field assistance, as appropriate. Makes arrangements for non-school use of playing fields and facilities, within district guidelines. Arranges field and gym practice schedules. Provides for physical examination of all athletes prior to the beginning of each season. Administers the insurance program covering school athletes and assumes responsibility for all processing of reports and claims. Plans and supervises an annual recognition program for school athletes. Regularly attends and represents the school at district and state meetings regarding athletics. Assists the administration in the implementation and supervision of the district’s policies regarding student, extra-curricular participation. Collects data and reports on student extra-curricular participation and requirements of Title IX. Helps to maintain physical facilities and communicates needs of the school’s athletic program to school and district administration. ADDITIONAL DUTIES: Performs other related tasks as assigned by the Principal, Superintendent or other district office administrator as designated by the Superintendent. QUALIFICATIONS: Must hold a South Carolina teaching and/or administrative certification. Preference will be given to individuals with national certification in Athletic/Sports Administration. Valid driver’s license . EXPERIENCE : A minimum of five years of experience in coaching team sports, preferably at the high school level. At least five years of teaching experience preferred. PHYSICAL DEMANDS: The physical demands described here are representative of those that must be met by an employee to successfully perform the essential responsibilities and functions of the job and are not meant to be all inclusive. Reasonable accommodation may be made to enable individuals with disabilities to perform the essential responsibilities and functions of the job. Unless reasonable accommodations can be made, while performing this job the staff member shall: Use strength to lift items needed to perform the functions of the job. Sit, stand and walk for required periods of time. Speak and hear. Use close vision, color vision, peripheral vision and depth perception along with the ability to focus vision. Communicate effectively in English, using proper grammar and vocabulary. American Sign Language or Braille may also be considered as acceptable forms of communication. Reach with hands and arms and use hands and fingers to handle objects and operate tools, technology equipment, computers, and/or controls. WORK PLACE EXPECTATIONS: Work effectively with and respond to diverse cultures or backgrounds. Demonstrate professionalism and appropriate judgment in behavior, speech, and dress in a neat, clean and professional manner for the assignment and work setting. Have regular and punctual attendance. Follow all District policies, work procedures, and reasonable requests by the proper authorities. As an employee of Beaufort County School District, I am to adhere to confidentiality as directed by Federal, State and Local Laws/Regulations; and in accordance with district administrative rules as it pertains to sensitive information, employee information and student information. Failure to adhere to confidentiality may result in disciplinary actions, up to and including termination of employment. REPORTS TO: Principal DAYS PER YEAR: 220 SALARY CLASSIFICATION: Paid on the Administrative Salary Schedule - Level 207 EMPLOYEE GROUP: Administrative APPLICATION PROCEDURES: Must apply online by completing an "Administrative" application and adding this specific job to your online application. FLSA STATUS: Exempt EMPLOYEE'S ACKNOWLEDGEMENT STATEMENT: 1. I have reviewed the above position description and understand its contents. 2. I am aware that my position description may be revised or updated at any time and once notified of changes, I remain responsible for knowledge of its contents. 3. I hereby certify that I possess the physical and mental ability to fulfill the essential functions of the above position with or without reasonable accommodation(s). If I require accommodation(s) in order to fulfill any or all of these functions, I agree to provide information to the District regarding the requested accommodation(s). Employee's Name (Print): ___________________________________ Employee's Signature: ___________________________________ Date: __________________________ THE BEAUFORT COUNTY SCHOOL DISTRICT IS AN EQUAL OPPORTUNITY EMPLOYER

Rock Hill is looking for a Basketball coach to join our program. Apply now.
Reports To: School Athletic Director and/or School Administration Job Summary: Rock Hill Schools is seeking a dedicated and knowledgeable Athletic Coach to lead, motivate, and develop athletes to reach their highest potential in both skill and character. The Athletic Coach will plan and implement practice sessions, guide athletes in training and competition, promote teamwork and sportsmanship, and ensure the health and safety of all participants. Key Responsibilities: Develop and execute effective training programs and practice plans. Instruct athletes in the rules, techniques, and strategies of the sport. Evaluate athlete performance and provide constructive feedback. Foster a positive team culture that emphasizes discipline, effort, integrity, and mutual respect. Ensure adherence to all rules and regulations set by the school, league, or governing body. Monitor the physical and mental condition of athletes, including injury prevention and first response. Coordinate with assistant coaches, athletic trainers, and other staff. Communicate regularly with athletes, parents, and administration. Attend all practices, games, meetings, and required events. Maintain equipment and inventory related to the specific sport. Serve as a role model and mentor to student-athletes. Qualifications: Proven coaching experience in [specific sport, e.g., basketball, soccer, track, etc.]. Knowledge of sport-specific techniques, rules, and strategies. Excellent leadership, motivational, and organizational skills. Strong interpersonal and communication abilities. Satisfactory background report Preferred: Experience coaching at the [high school/collegiate/professional] level. Relevant coaching certification (e.g., NFHS, USA Coaching, etc.) Start Date: Determined by Human Resources. PRE-ONBOARDING REQUIREMENTS: As a condition of employment and prior to onboarding, the district will conduct a criminal history background check. The recommended candidate will be required to provide personal information necessary to complete the background check such as date of birth, gender, and race to remain in compliance with applicable laws and regulations. DISCLAIMER: The District reserves the right to modify job postings and/or job descriptions at any time to meet organizational needs. EEOC STATEMENT: Rock Hill Schools provides equal employment opportunities to all employees and applicants for employment and prohibits discrimination and harassment of any type without regard to race, color, religion, age, sex, national origin, disability status, genetics, protected veteran status, sexual orientation, gender identity or expression, or any other characteristic protected by federal, state, or local laws. This policy applies to all terms and conditions of employment, including recruiting, hiring, placement, promotion, termination, layoff, recall, transfer, leaves of absence, compensation, and training.

Job.JobTitle: Coaching - HS Boys Junior Varsity Head Volleyball Coach
Job.JobDescription:
Cincinnati City School District is looking for a Volleyball Head JV Coach to join our program. Apply now.
JOB RESPONSIBILITIES: Oversee all aspects of the Boys Junior Varsity (JV) Volleyball Team including player development, game strategies, and team leadership. Oversee all aspects of the JV Volleyball Program tryouts, practices, and games. Is knowledgeable about the game, particularly game management, and rules. Develop and execute an effective training program. Motivate and mentor student-athletes on and off the Court. Instruct and guide student-athletes in fundamental skills, strategies, and physical training necessary for them to realize a degree of individual and team success. Instruct and guide student-athletes to lead them to the formulation and enhancement of moral values, pride of accomplishment, acceptable social behavior, self-discipline, and self-confidence. Foster teamwork, discipline, respect, sportsmanship, and skills development. Ensure the safety and well-being of student-athletes. Cooperate, in a timely manner, with School Athletic Director and the District Athletic Department in regard to requests for information that might be pertinent and/or periodically required. Meet all the requirements mandated by the District Board of Education, The Ohio High School Athletic Association (OHSAA), and The Ohio Department of Education (ODE) relative to the mandated Coaches Tool Chest (CTC) Training/ Certification and the ODE Pupil Activity Permit (PAP). Check the status of academic eligibility for all prospective student-athletes. Follow the District Athletic Department Policies as outlined in the Coaches Handbook and meet all criteria as outlined in the job description. Model the behavior expected of our student-athletes including timeliness to practices and games, being positive and enthusiastic, being respectful, and practicing constructive criticism while upholding the disciplines of the program. Maintain a suitable sideline conduct, including the use of appropriate language, at all times. Accept and profit from constructive criticism and suggestions for improving coaching performance. Recognize the need to be aware of and implement all applicable administrative polices set forth by the District Board of Education, Superintendents of Schools, School Principals, Conference Leagues, ODE, and OHSAA. Provide all applicable in-season training rules as well as any that may apply to out-of-season conduct to student-athletes prior to the start of the season. Follow appropriate due process procedures when violations occur. Participate in Clinics and other available programs to improve coaching performance. Participate in School events (i.e., Awards Night, Pep Assemblies, Athletic Boosters Meetings, etc. Assist in ensuring that team is well-prepared for opponents. Maintain accurate inventory of equipment and supplies. SKILLS/QUALIFICATIONS: Fundraising experience. Strong knowledge of volleyball rules, techniques, and strategies. Must be passionate and dedicated to inspire student-athletes. Previous experience coaching volleyball preferred. Have the ability to motivate and inspire student-athletes. Serve as a positive role model. Demonstrated knowledge and skills associated with the sport. TRAINING/CERTIFICATIONS: Ohio Secondary Teaching Certificate preferred. Evidence of successful past coaching experience at the High School and/or College level preferred. Provide satisfactory result of County Police Records Check, State BCII Check, and a FBI Fingerprint Background Check. Possess an Ohio Department of Education (ODE) Pupil Activity Permit (PAP). Provide proof of completion of Coaches Tool Chest (CTC) Training/Certification. CONTRACT PROVISIONS: TYPE OF CONTRACT: Supplemental Annual Limited SALARY: Per Schedule E Contract of current C.F.T. Labor Agreement REPORTS TO: School Principal APPLICATION PROCEDURE: Apply online via CPS Unified Talent SELECTION PROCEDURE: Interview

Job.JobTitle: Coaching - HS Girls Varsity Head Track Coach
Job.JobDescription:
Cincinnati City School District is looking for a Track & Field Head Varsity Coach to join our program. Apply now.
Job Responsibilities: Oversee the entire Girls Track Program and serve as the Varsity Head Track Coach. Develop and train student-athletes to participate in competitive track. Develop and execute an effective training program. Monitor, motivate, supervise, and mentor student-athletes on and off the Track. Ensure the safety and well-being of student-athletes. Instruct and guide student-athletes in fundamental skills, strategies, and physical training necessary for them to realize a degree of individual and team success. Instruct and guild student-athletes to lead them to the formulation and enhancement of moral values, pride of accomplishment, acceptable social behavior, self-discipline, and self-confidence. Foster teamwork, sportsmanship, and skills development. Schedule a full season of contests including State-Tournament Track Meets. Work cooperatively and in a timely manner, with School Athletic Office and CPS District Athletics Department, in regard to all requests for information that might be periodically required. Check the status of academic eligibility for all prospective student-athletes. Follow District Athletic Department Policy as outlined in Coaches Handbook and meet all criteria as outlined in job description. Meet all requirements set forth by the State Board of Education, CPS, the Ohio High School Athletic Association (OHSAA), and The Ohio Department of Education (ODE) relative to the mandated Coaches Tool Chest (CTC) and ODE Pupil Activity Permit (PAP). Recognize the need to be aware of and implement all applicable administrative policies set forth by the Board of Education, Superintendents of Schools, School Principals, Conference Leagues, OHSAA. Provide all applicable in-season training rules as well as any that may apply to out-of-season conduct to team members in writing prior to the start of the season. Follow appropriate due process procedures when violations occur. Model the behavior expected of our student-athletes including timeliness to games and practices. being positive and enthusiastic, being respectful, and practicing constructive criticism while upholding the disciplines of the program. Be appropriately dressed at all times. Maintain suitable sideline conduct, including the use of appropriate language, at all times. Accept and profit from constructive criticism and suggestions for improving coaching performance. Participate in clinics and other available programs to improve coaching performance. Participate in School Events (i.e., Awards Night Programs, Pep Assemblies, Athletic Boosters Meetings, etc.). Show physical vitality for the sport as well as use of good judgement and decision-making. Be competent in techniques of the sport so that student-athletes respond positively to directions given. Demonstrate the ability to adapt properly to changing game situations. Be well-versed and knowledgeable in matters pertaining to the sport and look at ways to constantly improve knowledge and coaching techniques. Be innovative in using new coaching techniques and ideas in addition to already proven methods of coaching. Ensure that team is well-prepared for opponents. Maintain accurate inventory of equipment and supplies. Skills/Essential Functions: Fundraising experience. Previous experience coaching track is required. Have a passion and dedication to motivate and inspire student-athletes. Ability to serve as a positive role model. Demonstrated knowledge of skills associated with this sport. Ability to work effectively in a multicultural environment. Training/Certifications: Ohio Secondary Teaching Certificate preferred. Successful participation in a similar activity in high school and/or college. Provide a satisfactory result on a County Police Records Check, State BCII Check, and FBI Fingerprint Background Check. Possess an Ohio Department of Education (ODE) Pupils Activity Permit (PAP). Provide proof of completion of Coaches Tool Chest (CTC) Training/Certification Contract Provisions: Type of Contract: Supplemental Annual Limited Salary: Per Schedule E of current C.F.T. Labor Agreement Reports To: Principal Application Procedure: Apply Online Selection Procedure: Interview

Meet all the requirements mandated by the District Board of Education, The Ohio High School Athletic Assn. (OHSAA) and the Ohio Department of Education (ODE) relative to mandated coaching requirements (Coaches Tool Chest (CTC) Training/Certification and Pupil Activity Permit (PAP). Check the status of academic eligibility for all prospective student-athletes. Follow the District Athletic Department Policies as outlined in the Coaches Handbook and meet all criteria as outlined in the job description. Model the behavior expected of student-athletes including timeliness to practices and games, being positive and enthusiastic, being respectful, and practicing constructive criticism while upholding the disciplines of the program. Maintain a suitable sideline conduct, including the use of appropriate language, at all times. Recognize the need to be aware of, and implement, all applicable administrative policies set forth by the District Board of Education, Superintendents of Schools, School Principals, Conference Leagues, ODE, and OHSAA. Provide all applicable in-season training rules as well as any that may apply to out-of-season. Follow appropriate due process procedures when violations occur. Participate in School events (i.e. Pep Assemblies, Awards Night, Athletic Boosters Meeting, etc. Ensure that teams are well prepared for opponents. Ensure the safety and well-being of student-athletes. Maintain accurate inventory of equipment and supplies. Skills/Qualifications: Fundraising experience. Strong leadership. Must be passionate and dedicated to inspire student-athletes. Previous throwing experience required. Serve as a role model. Demonstrated knowledge and skills associated with the sport. Training/Certifications: Ohio Secondary Teaching Certificate preferred. Evidence of successful past coaching experience required. Provide satisfactory result of County Police Records Check, State BCII Check, and a Fingerprint Background Check. Possess an Ohio Department of Education (ODE) Pupil Activity Permit (PAP). Provide satisfactory proof of completion of Coaches Tool Chest (CTC) Training/Certification. Contract Provisions: Type of Contract: Supplemental Annual Limited Salary: Per Schedule E Contract of current C.F.T Labor Agreement Reports To: School Principal Application Procedure: Apply online via CPS Unified Talent Selection Procedure: Interview
Job.SalaryRange: Per Schedule E Contract of current C.F.T Labor Agreement

Twinsburg City is looking for a Volleyball Volunteer Assistant Coach to join our program. Apply now.
SUPPLEMENTAL COACHING POSITION (Varsity) QUALIFICATIONS: 1. Has the ability to organize and supervise a total sports program. 2. Has previous successful coaching experience in assigned sport. 3. The coach must have substantial knowledge of the technical aspects of the sport and, at the same time, must continue to examine new theories and procedures pertinent to the field. 4. The coach must serve as an appropriate role model for student athletes through his/her instructions and actions. Must Have : Pupil Activity Permit American Red Cross or American Heart Association training. First Aide Training Concussion Protocol Course REPORTS TO: The athletic director, who provides overall objectives and final evaluation in conjunction with the high school administration JOB GOAL: To instruct athletes in the fundamental skills, strategy and physical training necessary for them to realize a degree of individual and team success. At the same time, the coach must instruct and demonstrate behaviors that lead to socially acceptable character development, self-discipline, self-confidence, and pride of accomplishment in the student athletes. To upgrade his/her knowledge and skills through coaching clinics, observations, consultation, etc. GENERAL: 1. The success of athletic programs has a strong influence on the community's image of the entire system. The public exposure is a considerable responsibility and community/parent pressure for winning performance is taxing, but must not override the objectives of good sportsmanship and good mental health. 2. The position includes other unusual aspect such as extended time, risk injury factor and due process predicaments. 3. It is the express intent of this job description to give sufficient guidance to function. In cases not specifically covered, it shall be assumed that a coach shall exercise common sense and good judgment. DUTIES AND RESPONSIBILITIES: 1. Has a thorough knowledge of all the athletic policies approved by the Twinsburg Board of Education. Schools' Board of Education and is responsible for their implementation by the entire staff of the sports program. 2. Has knowledge of existing system, state and league regulations; implements same consistently and interprets them for staff. STAFF RESPONSIBILITIES: 4. Establish the fundamental philosophy, skills and techniques to be taught by staff. Designs conferences, clinics and staff meetings to insure staff awareness of overall program. 5. Trains and informs staff, encourages professional growth by encouraging clinic attendance according to local clinic policy. 6. Delegates specific duties, supervises implementation and, at season's end, analyzes staff effectiveness and evaluates all assistants. 7. Maintains discipline, and works to increase morale and cooperation. 8. Performs such other duties which may be assigned by the athletic director/principal. ADMINISTRATIVE DUTIES: 9. Assists the athletic director in scheduling, providing transportation and requirements for tournament and special sport events. 10. Assists in the necessary preparation to hold scheduled sport events or practices and adheres to scheduled facility times. Coordinates program with maintenance and school employees. 11. Provides documentation to fulfill state and system requirements concerning physical examinations, parental consent and eligibility. 12. Provides proper safeguards for maintenance and protection of assigned equipment sites. 13. Advises the athletic director and recommends policy, method or procedural changes. STUDENT RESPONSIBILITIES : 14. Serves as a mentor to each athlete to promote the development and demonstration in student athletes the character traits of trustworthiness, respect, responsibility, fairness, caring, and citizenship. 15. Gives constant attention to a student athlete's grades and conduct. 16. By his/her presence at all practices games and while traveling, provides assistance, guidance and safeguards for each participant. 17. Provides training rules and any other unique regulations of the sport to each athlete who is considered a participant. 18. Initiates programs and policies concerning injuries, medical attention and emergencies through collaboration with athletic trainer. 19. Completes paperwork on all disabling athletic injuries on proper forms and submits to athletic trainer's office within 24 hours. 20. Directs student managers, assistants and statisticians. 21. Assists athletes in their college or advanced educational planning. FINANCE AND EQUIPMENT: 22. Participates in the budgeting function with the athletic director by establishing requirements for the next season. Recommends equipment guidelines as to type, style, color or technical specifications. Is responsible for operating within budget appropriations. 23. Is accountable for all equipment and collects the cost of any equipment lost or not returned. Arranges for issuing, storing and reconditioning of equipment and submits annual inventory and current records concerning same. 24. Properly marks and identifies all equipment before issuing or storing. 25. Monitors equipment rooms and coaches' offices, authorizes who may enter, issue or requisition equipment. 26. Permits the athletes to only be in authorized areas of the building at the appropriate times. 27. Examines locker rooms before and after practices and games, checking on general cleanliness of the facility. Responsible for cleanliness and maintenance of specific sport equipment. 28. Secures all doors, lights, windows and locks before leaving building if custodians are not on duty. 29. Instills in each player a respect for equipment and school property, its care and proper use. PUBLIC RELATIONS: 30. Organizes parents, coaches, players and guests for preseason meetings. 31. Promotes the sport within the school through recruiting athletes who are not in another sports program and promotes the sport outside the school through news media, community youth programs, or in any other feasible manner. 32. Responsible for the quality, effectiveness and validity of any oral or written release to local media. 33. Responsible for maintaining good public relations with news media, booster club, parents, officials, volunteers and fans. 34. Presents information to news media concerning schedules, tournaments and results.

Provides proper safeguards for maintenance and protection of assigned equipment sites. 5. Serves as a mentor to each athlete to promote the development and demonstration in student athletes the character traits of trustworthiness, respect, responsibility, fairness, caring, and citizenship. 6. Gives constant attention to a student athlete's grades and conduct. 7. By his/her presence at all practices games and while traveling, provides assistance, guidance and safeguards for each participant. 8. Provides training rules and any other unique regulations of the sport to each athlete who is considered a participant. 9. Helps to initiate programs and policies concerning injuries, medical attention and emergencies. 10. Participates in the budgeting function with the head coach by establishing requirements for the next season. 11. Assists the head coach and properly marks and identifies all equipment before issuing or storing. 12. Supervises athletes and makes sure that they are in authorized areas at the appropriate times. 13. Examines locker rooms before and after practices and games, checking on general cleanliness of the facility. Responsible for cleanliness and maintenance of specific sport equipment. 14. Instills in each player a respect for equipment and school property, its care and proper use. 15. Promotes the sport within the school through recruiting athletes who are not in other sports. 16. Performs such other duties which may be assigned by the athletic director/principal.

Job.JobDescription:
Indian Hill High School is looking for a Volleyball Athletics/Activities/ to join our program. Apply now.
HEAD VOLLEYBALL COACH – POSITION POSTING Indian Hill Exempted Village School District To Apply: Email Brian Phelps , Athletic Director, with a resume and letter of interest . POSITION: Head Volleyball Coach LOCATION: Indian Hill High School STARTING DATE: Spring 2026 Season CATEGORY: Supplemental REPORTS TO: Athletic Director STATUS: FLSA Exempt; Section 3319.02 – ORC GENERAL DESCRIPTION Indian Hill High School is ready to welcome an inspiring leader to take the helm of a volleyball program bursting with potential, energy, and pride. Our student-athletes are competitive, committed, and hungry to grow — and they thrive in a district where excellence isn’t just encouraged, it’s expected. As the Head Volleyball Coach, you’ll shape a program that blends high-level skill development with culture-building, leadership, and the kind of team chemistry that makes something magical happen on and off the court. This is your chance to build a marquee volleyball experience in one of Ohio’s most admired school districts — a place where families are supportive, the athletic department is collaborative, and student-athletes buy in fully to the vision of a coach who leads with purpose. From practice strategy to game-day intensity, you’ll have the opportunity to elevate the entire program and create moments that athletes and fans will remember for years. If fueling competitive success, developing strong young leaders, and building a program rooted in heart, grit, and high expectations excites you — Indian Hill is ready for you to lead the charge. Indian Hill High School is a high-achieving Division II athletic program serving approximately 650 students in grades 9–12. Candidates must possess all required Ohio Department of Education coaching credentials and successfully complete FBI/BCI background checks. PREFERRED SKILLS • Prior volleyball coaching experience preferred • Understanding of systems, player development, and strategy • Ability to build positive team culture

Central York is looking for a Field Hockey Head Coach to join our program. Apply now.
The Head Middle School Field Hockey Coach is responsible for leading and developing a program that fosters skill growth, teamwork, and a love for the game. This role involves creating a positive, inclusive, and structured environment where student-athletes can build confidence, sportsmanship, and a strong work ethic. The head coach will know the rules of the game and instruct participants using current and appropriate coaching methods and strategies that promote good health and safety practices, physical fitness, sportsmanship, and skill development. Primary Duties and Responsibilities: Promote, select, supervise and instruct student-athletes on accordance with established District, Conference and PIAA standards and regulations. Team selection is subject to the Athletic Director's review and approval. Know the rules of the game and instruct participants using current and appropriate coaching methods and strategies that promote good health and safety practices, physical fitness, sportsmanship, and skill development. Project a positive model for student-athletes, the general student body, and the community at large through appearance, positive attitude and enthusiastic personality, emotional maturity, self-control, leadership, planning, preparation and good sportsmanship. Maintain effective communications with students, parents, staff, administration, the media and higher institutions of higher education. Explain the expectations of the head coach to each assistant coach as it pertains to performance and the evaluation of performance. Conduct and attend meetings as mandated by the PIAA, the Conference, and as assigned by the Athletic Director or assigned principal. Plan and schedule, in coordination with the Athletic Director, all practices and recommend use of facilities. Maintain accurate and timely records as mandated by the PIAA, the Conference, and as directed by the Athletic Director. Acquire and maintain First Aid and CPR certification yearly (provided by Central York School District), and administer emergency treatment of injuries in the absence of a trainer, physician, or other person more qualified than him/herself. Safeguard all equipment and facilities to assure safety and proper use. Maintain an up-to-date and accurate inventory of all equipment and supplies used in the sport. Annually prepare and submit a detailed requisition for equipment and supplies needed for the following year to the Athletic Director. Promote positive self-esteem and climate among all participants and provide for appropriate recognition of student-athlete achievement in accordance with District, Conference and PIAA standards and practices. Promote appropriate use of language. Complete additional duties as assigned by the Athletic Director or assigned principal Where : Central York Middle School 1950 North Hills Road York, PA 17406 Qualifications: * Combination of education and experience necessary to perform the tasks outlined in this job description * Must have (or willing to obtain) current PA Criminal, PA Child Abuse and FBI Fingerprint History Background Clearances.

Job.JobTitle: EDEP: 2025-26 Tennis [Boys] - MS - Head Coach - Posting #1759
Job.JobDescription:
Cheltenham is looking for a Tennis Head Coach to join our program. Apply now.
EQUAL OPPORTUNITY EMPLOYER 11.11.25 Cheltenham School District announces the following extra duty-extra pay position(s) is available for the 2025-2026 school-year, at Cedarbrook Middle School: Tennis [Boys] - Head Coach Must have extensive knowledge of the sport and the ability to effectively teach and communicate fundamental skills. Must have at least 2 - 4 years of coaching experience working with children and/or adolescents; coaching at the high school level preferred. A strong understanding of the sport’s theory and practice is required; experience as a player or coach at the high school or collegiate level is preferred. Must demonstrate the personal qualities necessary to foster sportsmanship and support student-athlete success in a positive and encouraging environment. The ideal candidate will possess excellent communication, organizational, and collaboration skills, along with a proven ability to build relationships with student-athletes, parents, staff, and school administration. Applicants must be prepared to submit required background checks that are no more than one year old from the date of issue , in accordance with state law. All applicants: Must complete the on-line application to be considered for this position, which is located under Employment Opportunities at www.cheltenham.org . External candidates : If hired, you will be required to provide Acts 34, 114, and 151 clearances and other mandatory employment forms. CHELTENHAM SCHOOL DISTRICT 2000 ASHBOURNE ROAD, ELKINS PARK, PA 19027

Job.JobTitle: Middle School Field Hockey Head Coach
Job.JobDescription:
Fox Chapel Area is looking for a Field Hockey Head Coach to join our program. Apply now.
THE FOLLOWING POSITION IS AVAILABLE FOR THE 2026 FALL SPORTS SEASON. MIDDLE SCHOOL FIELD HOCKEY COACH The Fox Chapel Area School District is seeking a middle school field hockey coach for the 2026 fall sports season. Applicants must have strong knowledge of the sport, proven leadership/communication and interpersonal skills, knowledge of the rules and fundamentals of baseball, previous coaching experience and/or playing this sport at the high school level or above is preferred. Resume should reflect both coaching and professional experience/background. -Clearances: Act 34, Act 114, Act 151, Act 126 (upon hire)

JV-Football Coach, SRMS The Sandy Run Middle School Athletic Department has anopening for a JV-Football Coach for Fall, 2026. The candidate should have a desire to work with student athletes, be enthusiastic, patient, organized, mature, dependable and responsible. In addition, knowledge of the PIAA rules and playing or coaching experience in football is preferred.

Job.JobTitle: Head Boys Tennis Coach - Cleveland HS - 25/26 SY - District Athletics
Job.JobDescription:
Cleveland HS is looking for a Head Varsity Coach to join our program. Apply now.
Posting Timeline This job is open until filled. Applications will be reviewed as received. Portland Public Schools reserves the right to make a hiring decision at any point during the posting period. Position Details DISTRICT MISSION: At PPS, our goal is that by the end of elementary, middle and high school every student by name is meeting or exceeding academic standards and is fully prepared to make productive life decisions. DISTRICT PROFILE: To obtain more information about our district, schools, learning environment and programs, please go to https://www.pps.net/Domain/265. Key points of the job description for a Varsity Head Coach: High ethical and professional standards. Direct a successful grade 9-12 program and oversee all facets of an entire community youth program. 3 years of High School coaching experience Be a role model for high school student-athletes. Cooperate with other school-sponsored sports by encouraging student-athletes to compete in other programs. Develop and implement an off-season program. Lead student-athletes in the support of other Portland Public Schools athletics and activities. The Head Coach will be responsible for the following: Manage all aspects of the high school program including training, practice schedules and routines. Hire and oversee a staff of assistant coaches Provide regular communication with parents/families Regularly track the academic progress of all student athletes in the program and facilitate necessary supports to ensure their academic eligibility. Cooperate with other school-sponsored sports and encourage athletes to compete in other programs. Develop and implement an off-season program for athletics. All new hired coaches will need to complete the following prior to employment: NFHS/ASEP Certification First Aid Card (CPR and AED trained) District Fingerprinting/Criminal check Completion of OSAA Concussion Test Completion of ODE Steroid Test Completion of OSAA Heat Test *Head Up Football *Cheer and Dance/Drill Spirit Safety Training Completion of Portland Public School District paperwork and trainings. Applicant will be asked to apply online at our website and include the following: A cover letter detailing your experience. A current resume. A minimum of three letters of recommendation/reference. All PPS job offers are contingent on the satisfactory completion of a background check, including fingerprinting and criminal records. Salary Range - $6100.00 to $10,000.00 * There is no compensation for summer and out of season workouts. Portland Public Schools (PPS) is seeking talented people from diverse backgrounds and experiences to lead change and inspire PPS students. At PPS, every employee, despite having different roles, is an educator. We hope to attract talented educators who model the core PPS Educator Essentials. With the District’s focus on eliminating systemic racism and its adverse impact on student learning, we seek to hire individuals who bring to our district a deep commitment to racial equity and social justice. The District is committed to equal opportunity and nondiscrimination in all its educational and employment activities. The District prohibits discrimination based on perceived or actual race; national or ethnic origin; color; sex; religion; age; sexual orientation; gender expression or identity; pregnancy; marital status; veteran’s status; familial status; economic status or source of income; mental or physical disability or perceived disability; or military service. 504 Coordinator: Marquita Guzman,mguzman@pps.net, 971-393-8633 ADA Coordinator: Jey Buno, jbuno@pps.net, 503-916-3360 Civil Rights Director: Dr. Bobby Cruz, bcruz@pps.net, 503-839-3660 Accessibility Statement Conflict/Complaint Resolution Process
